Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class RestClient.Builder

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.RestClient.Builder
Enclosing class:
RestClient
public static final class RestClient.Builder extends Object
Fluent builder for RestClient.

Beta — API subject to change.

Since:
9.2.1

    Adds one or more lifecycle interceptors called before/after each request.

    Sets the logger called at the end of every request (success or failure).

    Appends parsers (built into a ParserSet at build time if no set was supplied).
    RestClient.Builder

    Adds default query parameters appended to every request URL.

    rootUrl(String value)
    Sets a root URL prefix applied to all relative request URLs (those without ://).
    RestClient.Builder

    Appends serializers (built into a SerializerSet at build time if no set was supplied).
    RestClient.Builder

    Sets the HTTP transport to use.

  • Method Details

    • transport

      public RestClient.Builder transport(HttpTransport value)
      Sets the HTTP transport to use.

      If not set, RestClient discovers a transport via ServiceLoader. When no sibling transport module is on the classpath, the built-in JavaHttpTransport (backed by the JDK's HttpClient) is used as the default.

      Parameters:
      value - The transport. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • rootUrl

      public RestClient.Builder rootUrl(String value)
      Sets a root URL prefix applied to all relative request URLs (those without ://).
      Parameters:
      value - The root URL (e.g. "https://api.example.com/v1"). May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • headers

      public RestClient.Builder headers(HttpHeader... value)
      Adds default headers sent with every request.
      Parameters:
      value - The headers to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • header

      public RestClient.Builder header(String name, String value)
      Adds a default header sent with every request.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      value - The header value (eager). May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • header

      public RestClient.Builder header(String name, Supplier<String> value)
      Adds a default header with a lazy value, evaluated at each request.
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      value - Supplier for the header value. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • queryData

      public RestClient.Builder queryData(HttpPart... value)
      Adds default query parameters appended to every request URL.
      Parameters:
      value - The parts to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • queryData

      public RestClient.Builder queryData(String name, String value)
      Adds a default query parameter appended to every request URL.
      Parameters:
      name - The parameter name. Must not be null.
      value - The parameter value (eager). May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • interceptors

      public RestClient.Builder interceptors(RestCallInterceptor... value)
      Adds one or more lifecycle interceptors called before/after each request.
      Parameters:
      value - The interceptors to add. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • logger

      public RestClient.Builder logger(RestLogger value)
      Sets the logger called at the end of every request (success or failure).
      Parameters:
      value - The logger. May be null to disable logging.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • bodyConverter

      public RestClient.Builder bodyConverter(BodyConverter<?>... value)
      Prepends custom body converters to the default converter list.

      Custom converters are checked before the defaults when RestRequest.body(Object) is called.

      Parameters:
      value - The converters to prepend. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • bodyConverters

      public RestClient.Builder bodyConverters(BodyConverter<?>... value)
      Replaces the entire body converter list (including defaults).

      Use this when you want full control over body conversion, including disabling the built-in defaults.

      Parameters:
      value - The complete replacement converter list. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • serializers

      public RestClient.Builder serializers(SerializerSet value)
      Sets the serializer registry used for outbound bodies.
      Parameters:
      value - The serializer set. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • parsers

      public RestClient.Builder parsers(ParserSet value)
      Sets the parser registry used for inbound bodies.
      Parameters:
      value - The parser set. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • serializer

      public RestClient.Builder serializer(Serializer... value)
      Appends serializers (built into a SerializerSet at build time if no set was supplied).
      Parameters:
      value - The serializers to append. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • parser

      public RestClient.Builder parser(Parser... value)
      Appends parsers (built into a ParserSet at build time if no set was supplied).
      Parameters:
      value - The parsers to append. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • defaultSerializer

      public RestClient.Builder defaultSerializer(Serializer value)
      Designates the default serializer used for outbound bodies when no registered serializer matches the requested media type.

      This is the explicit, opt-in way to restore a fallback serializer. Without it, a request that cannot be matched to a registered serializer (and any body requiring serialization on a serializer-less client) fails rather than silently defaulting to JSON. Set defaultSerializer(JsonSerializer.DEFAULT) to recover the pre-10.0 implicit-JSON behavior.

      Parameters:
      value - The default serializer. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • defaultParser

      public RestClient.Builder defaultParser(Parser value)
      Designates the default parser used when the response Content-Type is absent or matches no registered parser.

      This is the explicit, opt-in way to restore a fallback parser. Without it, a response whose Content-Type matches no registered parser fails with 415 Unsupported Media Type rather than silently parsing as JSON. Set defaultParser(JsonParser.DEFAULT) to recover the pre-10.0 implicit-JSON behavior.

      Parameters:
      value - The default parser. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.

    • build

      public RestClient build()
      Builds and returns the RestClient.
      Returns:
      A new instance. Never null.
